videos.title: OAuth 2.0 explained with examples
videos.description: Welcome to the ultimate guide on OAuth 2.0! In this 10-minute video, we'll unravel the complexities of OAuth 2.0, exploring its fundamental workings, identity providers, access tokens, the four types of OAuth flows, and its integration with JWT. Whether you're a seasoned developer or new to the world of digital security, this video will equip you with the knowledge you need.

videos.title: What is SAML? A Comprehensive Guide with Examples
videos.description: Welcome to the comprehensive guide on SAML! In this 10-minute video, we'll unravel the complexities of SAML, exploring its fundamental workings, service providers, identity providers, , its use cases, and comparing it with OAuth.. Whether you're a seasoned developer or new to the world of digital security, this video will equip you with the knowledge you need.

videos.title: JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) explained with examples | System Design
videos.description: In this 10-minute video, I will explain what JSON Web Tokens (JWTs) are and how they work. I will also discuss the benefits of using JWTs, and how they can be used in distributed systems and microservices architecture.

videos.title: HTTPS Security | SSL | TLS | Network Protocols | System Design
videos.description: In this video I explain the everything you need to know about HTTPS and Security in the context of System Design Interviews

videos.title: SCIM: The Identity Protocol You Use Every Day (Without Knowing It) 🤫
videos.description: Every time a new employee joins your company, someone manually adding them to Gmail, Slack, HR, finance tools… sounds painful, right? That’s where SCIM (System for Cross-domain Identity Management) shines. In this video, we break down how SCIM simplifies identity provisioning and syncing across hundreds of apps—at scale. You’ll learn how giants like Netflix and Airbnb build SCIM Gateways and Identity Sync Services to centralize identity updates, use Kafka and webhooks for real-time distribution, and decouple internal systems without duplicating SCIM logic. From REST API patterns to federation and SSO, this is your go-to guide for modern identity management architecture.

videos.title: Zero Trust Security (ZTS) Explained: A Beginner's Guide for 2024
videos.description: Conquer cyber threats in today's evolving landscape with Zero Trust Security (ZTS)!  This video dives deep into the ZTS model,  a powerful approach that moves beyond traditional perimeter defenses.

videos.title: API Authentication: JWT, OAuth2, and More
videos.description: In this video, we dive into API Authentication, covering why it’s essential, how it secures API access, and four common methods: Basic Authentication, API Key Authentication, JWT Authentication, and OAuth. We’ll explore each method’s advantages and trade-offs and explain the crucial difference between authentication and authorization. Perfect for developers, this guide will help you understand how to secure your APIs effectively.

videos.title: 🔓 Mastering CORS: How to Securely Enable Cross-Origin Requests
videos.description: CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) is one of the most frustrating challenges developers face when working with REST APIs. You make a request, and suddenly, the browser blocks it with the dreaded "No ‘Access-Control-Allow-Origin’ header" error. But why does this happen, and how do you fix it without compromising security?

videos.title: ❌ XSS Attacks Explained – How HACKERS steal data with one line of code
videos.description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) is one of the most common and dangerous web vulnerabilities, capable of hijacking user sessions, stealing credentials, and injecting malicious scripts into trusted websites. In this video, we’ll explore the three major types of XSS attacks—Stored, Reflected, and DOM-Based—and show real-world examples of how hackers exploit them. We’ll also cover famous XSS exploits like the MySpace Samy Worm and PayPal’s XSS Vulnerability, proving why this attack vector remains a serious threat today. More importantly, you’ll learn how to prevent XSS in your own applications using input sanitization, escaping, Content Security Policy (CSP), and JavaScript security libraries like DOMPurify. By the end of this video, you'll have a developer’s playbook for identifying, mitigating, and defending against XSS attacks. Whether you're a developer, ethical hacker, or security enthusiast, this guide will give you the tools to secure your applications against malicious scripts.

videos.title: 💀 CSRF Attacks: How Hackers Can Hijack Your Users’ Accounts!
videos.description: CSRF attacks can trick users into performing unintended actions, from transferring money to changing account settings, without their knowledge. In this video, we break down how CSRF works, explore real-world attack examples (like the Twitter CSRF worm and GitHub vulnerability), and walk through the best ways to protect your web applications. You’ll learn how CSRF tokens, SameSite cookies, and CORS can defend against these attacks, with practical code examples in JavaScript, Node.js, and Spring Boot. Security is critical—don’t let your app be the next victim! 🚀

videos.title: ⚠️ SQL Injection: The HACK That Can Ruin Your Database in Seconds!
videos.description: SQL Injection is one of the most dangerous and widely exploited web vulnerabilities. With just a few keystrokes, attackers can steal entire databases, manipulate records, and gain unauthorized access to systems. In this video, we’ll break down SQL Injection in detail—how it works, real-world hacking incidents, and how to secure your applications from this devastating attack. You'll see live examples, including classic and blind SQL Injection techniques, and learn how hackers manipulate database queries. More importantly, we’ll show you how to prevent SQL Injection using prepared statements, input validation, and security best practices in Java (Spring Boot). Whether you're a backend developer, cybersecurity enthusiast, or just curious about how hackers break into systems, this video will give you everything you need to know. Don’t wait until it’s too late—protect your databases now! 🚀🔒

videos.title: FusionAuth System Design | Build Auth That You Actually Own
videos.description:
Most auth services are black boxes. FusionAuth is different. Check it out here: https://fnf.dev/4jiQynj
In this video, we break down the system design of FusionAuth, a modern CIAM platform that’s developer-first, self-hostable, API-driven, and refreshingly free of fluff.

videos.title: HTTP Secure Headers for Web App Security | CORS, CSP, HSTS and more
videos.description:
Most developers focus on securing their backend — but there's a powerful and often overlooked frontend layer: **HTTP Secure Headers**.
These headers quietly enforce browser-level security that protects against XSS, clickjacking, sniffing, and more — all without touching your frontend code.

videos.title: User Authentication Explained with SuperTokens and ReactJS
videos.description: Authentication is more than just login forms and passwords — it’s the front door to your app's security and scalability.

videos.title: I Got Fooled By a Scam Even as a Security Expert!
OAuth is Broken Without This | Meet PKCE
videos.description: If you're building a mobile app, single-page app (SPA), or browser-based tool that uses OAuth — PKCE is essential.

videos.title: What Happens When You Login? | IAM Secrets Used by Big Tech
videos.description: Identity and Access Management (IAM) is the invisible engine behind every secure login — from GitHub to Google Workspace to AWS. But how does it all really work?

videos.title: How SSH Works | Keys, Encryption & Real-World Examples
videos.description: SSH (Secure Shell) is the gold standard for securely accessing and managing remote servers. In this video, we’ll break down how SSH works under the hood, why it replaced Telnet, and how developers and DevOps teams use it every day.

videos.title: Single Sign-On (SSO) Explained in 10 Minutes | SAML, OIDC & SCIM
videos.description: Logging into multiple apps every day is frustrating — and risky. That’s where Single Sign-On (SSO) comes in. With one login, you can securely access everything — from email to HR systems to cloud dashboards.

videos.title: What EXACTLY is Bastion? | SSH Jump, Port Forwarding & Netflix
videos.description: Not every server can sit on the public internet — especially sensitive resources like production databases, app servers, or dashboards. But engineers still need access. That’s where **bastion hosts** come in.

videos.title: Passkeys Explained | Why Apple, Google & Microsoft Want to KILL Passwords
videos.description: Passwords have been the weakest link in security for decades — stolen, phished, reused, and dumped in breaches. But now Apple, Google, and Microsoft are betting on something better: **Passkeys**.

videos.title: How Databases Store Passwords Securely
videos.description:
What really happens to your password once you type it in?  
It doesn’t just sit inside a database waiting for hackers — at least, not if the system is built right.

videos.title: Private video
videos.description: This video is private.

videos.title: X.509 Certificates vs Vaults - The Security Industry Got It Wrong
videos.description: Why are we still using passwords and SSH keys stored in vaults when X.509 certificates have been securing the internet since the 1980s?

videos.title: They're Tracking Everything You Do (How to Stop It)
videos.description:

videos.title: How to Let AI Securely Access Your Database (RBAC + MCP)
videos.description: In this video, I show you how to securely connect AI agents like Claude to your application database using RBAC, MCP, and Clerk.

videos.title: Implementing OAuth and MFA: Full Authentication Breakdown
videos.description:
Every time you click “Sign in with Google” or “Connect with Twitter”, OAuth is working behind the scenes. But what is actually happening?
How does an app get access to your data without ever seeing your password?
And why do modern apps add an extra security layer with Multi-Factor Authentication?
